name: a rock

phase_0.boundary_condition: The crystalline lattice structure and the gravitational field holding the mass together.

VINIT.justification: The undifferentiated, molten state before cooling into a specific form.

TANCH.justification: The final state of solid matter that defines the rock's existence in space-time.

AFWD.justification: The forward process of pressure and heat transforming sediment into solid rock.

AREV.justification: The reverse process of breaking solid rock back down into constituent particles.

CLINK.justification: The sequential composition of geological events into a single body.

IMSCRIB.justification: The internal chemical identity that remains constant regardless of shape.

FSPLIT.justification: The force that splits a rock into its chemical components and physical fragments.

FFUSE.justification: The metamorphic process that reunites fragments and minerals into a single rock mass.

EVALT.justification: The successful maintenance of structural bonds.

EVALF.justification: The failure of structural bonds leading to breakage.

ENGAGR.justification: The state where a rock is simultaneously its old form and its new form during transition.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, irreversible record of an organism or event within the stone.

phase_3.void_description: The pre-tectonic state where no mineral identity has crystallized.
phase_3.true_description: A solid, stable state of geological equilibrium.
phase_3.false_description: A state of disintegration or structural failure.
phase_3.both_description: The plastic state of high-heat deformation where the rock is both solid and flowing.

steps.domain_action: Magmatic cooling begins the genesis of the lithic object.
The specific mineral composition identifies itself through crystallization.
Lithification transforms the cooling melt into a primary igneous body.
A trace mineral inclusion is permanently trapped, recording the initial cooling rate.
Tectonic stress creates a fault line, branching the rock into two potential paths.
The primary mass maintains cohesion despite the stress.
The rock undergoes successive layers of sedimentation on its surface.
Under extreme pressure, the rock enters a metamorphic state, being both its original and new form.
A surface fracture occurs due to thermal expansion.
Erosion begins to strip away the fractured surface layers.
Subduction and heat fuse the fractured and original components back into a unified metamorphic whole.
The rock recognizes its new, transformed identity.
A permanent deformation pattern is etched into the crystalline lattice.
The rock reaches a state of surface stability, anchored in the crust.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Tectonic and atmospheric forces translating energy into physical form.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Chemical bonding and electromagnetic forces between mineral grains.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The crystalline lattice and stratigraphic layers storing historical data.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Geological time scales (eons, eras, periods) governing the rate of change.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Periodic Table and the laws of thermodynamics.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Geothermal heat and gravitational potential energy.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Ordered crystalline structure or stable lithic mass.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle of the rock (Rock Cycle) conserves matter and energy through phase changes.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → AFWD → IFIX → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → ENGAGR → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: a rock
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=14
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(4, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑾  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑠 → 𐑭  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑾 → 𐑚  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [4] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [7] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def a_rock_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Magmatic cooling begins the genesis of the lithic object.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The specific mineral composition identifies itself through crystallization.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Lithification transforms the cooling melt into a primary igneous body.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [3] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (A trace mineral inclusion is permanently trapped, recording the initial cooli...)
  -- FSPLIT [4] (gran := 𐑚) (Tectonic stress creates a fault line, branching the rock into two potential paths.) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (The primary mass maintains cohesion despite the stress.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The rock undergoes successive layers of sedimentation on its surface.)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Under extreme pressure, the rock enters a metamorphic state, being both its o...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (A surface fracture occurs due to thermal expansion.)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Erosion begins to strip away the fractured surface layers.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Subduction and heat fuse the fractured and original components back into a unified metamorphic whole.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The rock recognizes its new, transformed identity.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (A permanent deformation pattern is etched into the crystalline lattice.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [13] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The rock reaches a state of surface stability, anchored in the crust.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def a_rock_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (a_rock_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def a_rock_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (a_rock_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem a_rock_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
